title: Skier dies after fall at Sugarbush
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A skier died after falling and sliding off a trail at Sugarbush Resort in Vermont on Saturday.
Vermont State Police were alerted to the incident on Stein’s Run, Lincoln Peak at 3:26 p.m.
An autopsy in Burlington will determine the cause of death; the man's name is withheld pending family notification.

title: As playoffs near, doubt the 2025-26 Bruins at your own risk
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins overcame a three-goal deficit to defeat Columbus Blue Jackets 4-3 in a shootout on Sunday.
Zacha and Arvidsson led the comeback with critical goals, enhancing Bruins' 92% odds of reaching the playoffs.
Bruins secured six points in a week, displaying resilience and toughness heading into the playoff season.

title: Dating back to the 1600s, the second-oldest home in Cambridge lists for $5.8M
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Hooper-Lee-Nichols House in Cambridge, built around 1685, is listed for sale at $5.8 million.
Owned by History Cambridge since 1957, the house served as its headquarters for 70 years.
Located on Brattle Street, the home has undergone Georgian, Victorian, and Colonial Revival alterations.

title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 32 points and had eight assists in the Celtics' 114-99 victory over the Hornets.
Payton Pritchard contributed 28 points and six assists, proving effective in his return to the starting lineup.
The Celtics faced the Hornets without Jaylen Brown and Derrick White, yet maintained a 2.5-game lead in conference standings.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics secured their 12th consecutive playoff berth with a 114-99 win over the Charlotte Hornets.
Despite being sidelined, Jaylen Brown celebrated the Celtics' 50th win in a season marked by Jayson Tatum's Achilles injury.
Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 32 points against Charlotte, highlighting his strong return from an Achilles tear.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early, 23, impresses for the Red Sox with a 2.19 ERA over five MLB starts.
Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run, five hits, and striking out six against the Reds.
Initially outside the rotation, Early replaced Johan Oviedo and excelled in his first 2026 season start.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet secured a Walter Cup Playoffs spot after defeating Minnesota Frost 4-2 at Grand Casino Arena.
Fleet's 4-game winning streak makes them the first team to clinch a playoff berth in the 2026 season.
First-year coach Kris Sparre led Boston to a 13-5-2-4 record, emphasizing strong goaltending and balanced scoring.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, is charged with manslaughter after a fatal stabbing in South Boston, police reported.
Police found a man with a stab wound at 258 Old Colony Ave; he died the following day at a hospital.
Initial assault charges against Pascual were upgraded to manslaughter; she faces arraignment in South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police shot a 44-year-old man who allegedly fired at officers after a vehicle pursuit early Sunday.
A 71-year-old man was grazed by a bullet during the shootout and both men sustained non-life threatening injuries.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating; a handgun with an obliterated serial number was found with the driver.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the NHL playoffs.
John Tortorella has been named as Cassidy's replacement after Vegas lost six of their last seven games.
The Golden Knights struggled with goalie depth this season despite high expectations and adding Mitch Marner.
